demographics.living_situation: lives at home; adult child present and helping

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach is so huge it is crushing my lungs, and I feel completely drained of all strength. (Adult child on the line adds: he's been really muddled and drowsy the last day or two.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The swelling has ballooned over the last couple of days and now he can't breathe unless he's propped up, and he's not himself — confused and exhausted.

symptom_narrative.onset: the belly swelling has been building for about two weeks
symptom_narrative.duration: about two weeks, and much worse over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: a huge, tight, heavy abdominal swelling that makes it hard to breathe, with profound weakness and drowsiness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has been — my belly feels like it is crushing my lungs and I have no strength left at all
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my whole belly, and it is affecting my breathing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting up or being propped on lots of pillows helps me breathe a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying flat makes the breathlessness much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: profound weakness and muscle wasting, feeling full very quickly when I eat and some discomfort under the left ribs, drowsiness and confusion my family has noticed, and my skin and the whites of my eyes have gone yellow
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I bled heavily from ruptured veins in my gullet (food pipe) about a year ago
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I am bedbound, propped up on multiple pillows just to keep breathing

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: advanced liver disease / cirrhosis with high pressure in the liver blood vessels (I was told the pressure backs up)
past_medical_history.how_managed: water tablets and a lactulose syrup

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: bled heavily from ruptured veins in my gullet about a year ago and was in hospital for it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with support from an adult child who made the call; hasn't managed to take the water tablets or the lactulose syrup for about a week because of the confusion and drowsiness

communication_style.typical_message_style: patient is drowsy and muddled; much of the history is being given by the adult child who is anxious and worried

objective_data_offered.value: his belly has swollen up massively over the last couple of weeks, much worse in the last two days — it looks hard and tight like a drum
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: visual impression from a family member over video/phone, not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of his eyes and his skin have turned yellow
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: colour impression over a video link, lighting-dependent

objective_data_offered.value: there are big veins bulging out on the skin around his belly button
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lay observation of surface veins, not a clinical sign assessment

objective_data_offered.value: he's been unusually drowsy, muddled, forgetful and not making much sense over the last day or two
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family's subjective account of behaviour change

objective_data_offered.value: he can't lie flat at all — he has to be propped up on pillows or sitting in the recliner or he can't breathe
he's put on a lot of weight very quickly with all the swelling
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported impression, not a measured home-scale value
